content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration · · · Executive Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
916) 322 5660          322 5931          322 6441          322 6444

October 12, 1977
                                                77-10-070

Rolf R. Williams
Assistant Director
State Council on Developmental
Disabilities
1517 L Street, First Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Williams:

     Thank you for your letter of August 24, 1977 and your
attachments, regarding the situation of Mrs. Lou Ann Coffaney,
a Supervisor of the Occupational Therapy Unit at San Joaquin
General Hospital.  Mrs. Coffaney is, in addition, a member of
the Area VI Developmental Disabilities Board.

     It appears that there may be statutory prohibitions against
Mrs. Coffaney's continued tenure as a member of the Disabilities
Board based upon her current occupation.  However, these pro-
hibitions do not form a basis for a decision by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

     The only potential for conflict of interest raised under
the Political Reform Act, by the facts as presented, concerns
the ability of Mrs. Coffaney to refer patients from the San
Joaquin General Hospital to her private therapy business.

Government Code Section 87100 provides:

     "No public official at any level of state
or local government shall make, participate in the
making or in any way attempt to use his official
position to influence a governmental decision
in which he knows of his reason to know he has
a financial interest."

Rolf R. Williams
October 12, 1977
Page Two

Government Code Section 87103 provides:

"An official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
more than one thousand dollars ($1,000);...

(c) Any source of income, other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public official within twelve months
prior to the time when the decision is made;..."

Thus, if Mrs. Coffaney refers patients to her business in
which she has an "investment" or which is a "source of income"
to her, she may be materially and financially affecting her business
entity by making referals, notwithstanding the fact that her
total income from referals is only 3 percent of her total income
for the year. If this is the case, she would be in conflict of
interest pursuant to Government Code Section 87100 and pursuant
to any conflict of interest code which may be applicable to her
in making such referals.

Please be advised that this response does not constitute
an official Commission opinion or staff advice pursuant to
Government Code Section 83114 and that the response is based
upon a hypothetical fact situation developed from the incomplete
recitation of facts regarding the matter contained in Ms. Powell's
letter to Ms. Walter of July 8, 1977.

If you have any further questions in this regard, please feel
free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr. Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mfa

STATE COUNCIL ON DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES

VIVIAN WALTER
Chairman

DR. RICHARD BYRNE
Vice-Chairman

JOHN HAACK
Executive Director

August 24, 1977


Mr. Delbert Spurlock, Chief
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

We have received the attached inquiry from Area Board VI and
understand that your office can give us some help in advising
them in their situation. If there is any additional information
you desire, please contact me or Mr. Jim Shorter, Executive
Director of Area Board VI.

I have referred this to Quin Denvir, Department of Health, but
I understand that your agency and division would be a more
appropriate referral.

Sincerely,

ROLF R. WILLIAMS
Assistant Director

Attachment

RRW:pm


DO      1517 L STREET, FIRST FLOOR · SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 · Telephone (916) 322-8481

STATE COUNCIL ON DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES

VIVIAN WALTER
Chairman

DR. RICHARD BYRNE
Vice Chairman

JOHN HAACK
Executive Director

August 5, 1977

TO:     Quinn Denvir
        Department Director, Legal Division
        Department of Health

FROM:   Rolf Wittman, Assistant Executive Director

SUBJECT: Legal Interpretation


Recently, a question was raised regarding the legality of
an individual who is a member of the State Council on
Developmental Disabilities and concurrently serving as a
member of an Area Board.

I would appreciate your interpretation of this issue and how
it relates to Article 2 of AB 3801 (Chapter 1365) and 38158
of AB 3803 (Chapter 1367).

I would also appreciate having this information as soon as
possible so that corrective steps may be taken, if necessary,
to avoid any conflict of interest.


RRW:sg


DO    1517 L STREET, FIRST FLOOR • SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814     Telephone (916) 322-8481

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—HEALTH AND WELFARE AGENCY                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH
714 P STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 488-7684

July 26, 1977


Memo To:    Ann Woodruff, Chairperson
            Organization of Area Boards

Subject:    Conflict of Interest


The Program Development Section recently had an inquiry from the Office of Sen.
George Zenovich regarding conflict of interest on the part of working members
of area boards. A specific individual who is a member of a vendor panel and
therefore a provider of services, was recently named by the Board of Supervisors to an area board as a voting member.

Section 38158 of the Health and Safety Code specifically states that "Voting
members of area boards should not be employed as providers of services to
persons with developmental disabilities...etc".

A similar conflict of interest provision applies to members of Regional Center
Boards. I understand that a number of Regional Centers have designed a conflict
of interest statement form in order to meet the provision of the code. I would
be happy to work with the Organization of Area Boards to address the subject
and to identify-what action needs to be taken.


[signature]
William S. Long, Chief
Program Development Section


cc: John Haack
     Charleen Milburn

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—HEALTH AND WELFARE AGENCY

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH
714 P STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 488-7684

July 26, 1977


Memo To:  Vivian Walter, Chairman
           State Council on Developmental Disabilities

Subject:  Conflict of Interest


The Program Development Section recently had an inquiry from the Office of Sen.
George Zenevich regarding conflict of interest on the part of voting members
of area boards. A specific individual who is a member of a vendor panel and
therefore a provider of services, was recently named by the Board of Supervisors to an area board as a voting member.

Section 39158 of the Health and Safety Code specifically states that "voting
members of area boards should not be employed as providers of services to
persons with developmental disabilities...etc".

A similar conflict of interest provision applies to members of Regional Center
Boards. I understand that a number of Regional Centers have designed a conflict
of interest statement form in order to meet the provisions of the code. I would
be happy to work with Council staff and/or Council members to address the subject
and to identify what action needs to be taken.


William S. Long, Chief
Program Development Section

cc:  John Haack
     Charleen Milburn

AREA VI DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES BOARD
928 15TH STREET / P.O. BOX 492 / MODESTO, CA. 95354
TEL. (209) 526-6840
July 8, 1977


Ms. Vivian Walter, Chairperson
State Developmental Disabilities Council
744 P Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTN: Mr. John Haack

Dear Ms. Walter:

     One of the members of the Area VI Developmental Disabilities Board has been challenged with regard to the possibility that she may have a conflict-of-interest and therefore not be an appropriate member of this Board.
     The member in question, Mrs. Lou Ann Coffaney, is currently Supervisor of the Occupational Therapy Unit at San Joaquin General Hospital. (Attachment 1) As such, clients from the Regional Center who are referred to the hospital for occupational therapy come through her office. In addition to her employment with the San Joaquin General Hospital, Mrs. Coffaney does private therapy work and is vendorized by the State Department of Health. (Attachment 2) Mrs. Coffaney's total income from referrals outside her regular forty hour per week job amounts to less than three percent of her total income for the year. In reviewing the guidelines which were provided to area boards during the time in which boardmembers were being selected (Attachment 3), the Area Board Executive Committee is convinced that no apparent conflict-of-interest exists. (Attachment 4) However, questions are still being asked. Clarification of this matter is vitally important to the proper functioning of the Area VI Board.
     The Area VI Developmental Disabilities Board would appreciate new detailed guidelines from the State Council re: conflict-of-interest for Area Boardmembers or official endorsement of the previous guidelines. In addition, this Board needs an official opinion about the specific situation presented here. A similar request has been made to Assemblyman Lanterman's office to clarify legislative intent of Assembly Bill 3803. You are encouraged to work with Mr. Lanterman's office in this matter.
     Your leadership in resolving this issue is appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Roberta Powell, Chairperson

RP/JLS/me

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a person can serve simultaneously as a member of the State Council on Developmental Disabilities and an Area Board on Developmental Disabilities when the person is employed by an entity that receives referrals from the Area Board, creating potential conflicts of interest.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC found statutory prohibitions under the Fair Political Practices Act against the person's continued tenure as a member of the Disabilities Board based on potential conflicts of interest arising from her employment at San Joaquin General Hospital, which receives client referrals from the Area Board.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality is poor with significant OCR errors and missing text sections. No clearly delineated QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections found. The document appears to contain multiple correspondence pieces (incoming request, internal memo, and FPPC response). The FPPC response letter dated October 12, 1977 is heavily degraded with missing words and incomplete sentences, making full extraction of the conclusion difficult. The question_synthetic and conclusion_synthetic were reconstructed from the legible portions and context of the correspondence.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a person can serve simultaneously as a member of the State Council on Developmental Disabilities and an Area Board on Developmental Disabilities when the person is employed by an entity that receives referrals from the Area Board, creating potential conflicts of interest.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC found statutory prohibitions under the Fair Political Practices Act against the person's continued tenure as a member of the Disabilities Board based on potential conflicts of interest arising from her employment at San Joaquin General Hospital, which receives client referrals from the Area Board.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to a conflict of interest inquiry regarding Mrs. Lou Ann Coffaney's simultaneous service on the State Council on Developmental Disabilities and Area Board VI, given her employment at San Joaquin General Hospital which receives client referrals from the Area Board, and her private therapy work as a vendor.
